commonPatterns.name: primary-action
commonPatterns.description: Single most important action on the page

commonPatterns.name: default-action
commonPatterns.description: Standard action — most commonly used variant

commonPatterns.name: icon-and-text
commonPatterns.description: Button with leading icon and text label

commonPatterns.name: icon-only
commonPatterns.description: Compact icon-only button for toolbars or inline actions

commonPatterns.name: destructive-confirmation
commonPatterns.description: High-impact or irreversible action requiring emphasis

commonPatterns.name: button-pair
commonPatterns.description: Primary + outline pairing for confirm/cancel flows

antiPatterns.scenario: Multiple primary buttons on one page
antiPatterns.reason: Confuses visual hierarchy and user decision-making
antiPatterns.alternative: Use one primary button; others should be outline or ghost

antiPatterns.reason: Buttons trigger actions, links navigate — mixing them breaks semantics and accessibility
The secondary variant is forbidden in this design system
antiPatterns.alternative: Use outline for standard actions or ghost for low-emphasis

antiPatterns.reason: Users must have clear visual warning before irreversible actions
antiPatterns.alternative: Always use the destructive variant for delete, remove, or irreversible operations

antiPatterns.reason: The lg size is forbidden. The system uses only xs, sm, and md. Three sizes are sufficient to cover all hierarchy and density needs.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use md for the most prominent action on the screen (header/top of page), sm as the default, or xs in compact spaces (tables, small cards, lists).

antiPatterns.scenario: Using md for general/secondary actions throughout the page
antiPatterns.reason: md is reserved for the most important action(s), typically in the header or top of the page. Overuse of md dilutes visual hierarchy.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use sm as the default for most actions. Reserve md for the primary hero action.

antiPatterns.scenario: Icon-only button that is not square in proportion
antiPatterns.reason: Icon-only buttons must always be square — equal width and height — to ensure consistent visual weight and alignment in toolbars, tables, and inline contexts.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the iconOnly prop, which collapses padding to produce a square button. Do not manually set width/height to non-square values.

semanticMeaning.primary: Single most important action on a page
semanticMeaning.link: Inline text-level action styled as a hyperlink

semanticMeaning.md: Reserved for the most important action(s) on the screen — typically in the header or top of the page. Do not use for general actions.
semanticMeaning.sm: The most commonly used size and the safe default when unsure which size to pick.
semanticMeaning.xs: Used in compact spaces: tables, small cards, compact lists, inline actions.

iconOnly.description: Renders as a square button for icon-only use. Icon-only buttons must always be square in proportion — equal width and height.

composition.asChild: Supports Radix Slot via asChild prop to render as a different element

interactions.hover: Subtle background shift; primary/outline/secondary gain elevation shadow
interactions.active: Translates down 0.5px and flattens shadow to simulate press

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Space/Enter to activate; Tab to navigate
accessibility.screenReader: Announces button label, variant is visual-only
accessibility.focusManagement: Visible 3px focus ring on focus-visible, ring color matches variant

rules: Only one primary button per page
Destructive actions must use destructive variant
Buttons may be used inline when action intent is clear
Never use the secondary variant — it is forbidden in this design system
Never use the lg size — it is forbidden. Only xs, sm, and md are allowed.
sm is the default and most common size. Use it when unsure.
md is reserved for the most important action(s) on the screen, usually in the header or top of the page. Do not use md for general actions.
xs is for compact spaces: tables, small cards, compact lists.
Icon-only buttons must be square in proportion — equal width and height. Always use the iconOnly prop.

aiHints.context: Use for any interactive action that changes state or triggers behavior. Default to outline variant unless the action is the single most important on the page (primary) or destructive. SIZE: sm is the default — use it unless you have a specific reason. md is reserved for the hero action at the top of the page. xs is for compact contexts (tables, cards, lists). lg is forbidden. Icon-only buttons must always be square (use the iconOnly prop).
